{"key_event":"Permanent Cold War national-security state","reasoning":"Federal power deepened through Korean War mobilization, intelligence and loyalty systems, highways, science funding, and civil-rights enforcement, though constitutional limits and federalism kept it short of total penetration. Civil society remained very strong through unions, churches, universities, civic associations, and the emerging civil-rights movement, but anti-communism, surveillance, and constrained dissent offset much of that gain.","society_change":0.1,"society_power":8.2,"state_change":0.3,"state_power":9.6}
